voterRegistration.deadline: Same-day registration available at the polls
voterRegistration.onlineDeadline: The day before the election via My Voter Page
voterRegistration.note: Vermont allows same-day voter registration at the polls. You can also register or update your registration online at My Voter Page (mvp.vermont.gov).

findYourDistrict.note: South Burlington is divided into multiple Vermont House districts. Use My Voter Page to look up your exact district and find your representatives.

southBurlingtonPollingLocations: Frederick H. Tuttle Middle School (Dorset Street)
Kevin Dorn Senior Center at City Hall (Market Street)

keyDates.description: Candidates may begin filing paperwork to appear on the primary ballot
All primary candidates must file by 5:00 PM to appear on the August ballot
Deadline for independent and third-party candidates to file petitions for the general election ballot
Voters in each party choose their nominees for the November general election
All South Burlington residents vote for Governor, legislature, U.S. House, and ballot measures

elections.name: Vermont Primary Election

absenteeBallot.note: Request an absentee ballot online through My Voter Page (mvp.vermont.gov)

elections.notes: Vermont has open primaries — you may request the ballot of any party regardless of your registration. You can change your party affiliation at the polls on Election Day.

elections.name: Vermont General Election

absenteeBallot.note: Vermont automatically mails ballots to all active registered voters. You may vote by mail or return your ballot in person.

elections.notes: Vermont automatically mails ballots to all registered voters for general elections. You can vote by mail, drop your ballot at a drop box, or vote in person at your polling place.
